Фрагмент: As you scrolled down, you noticed that bash wasn't very well ported, and that some functions didn't work.

Что найдено: Надёжные источники подтверждают общую идею, что Bash не полностью переносим между окружениями и что некоторые Bash-специфичные функции/«bashisms» могут не работать в других shell или в режиме POSIX. Однако формулировка "bash wasn't very well ported" слишком расплывчата и не является стандартным фактом; корректнее говорить о проблемах переносимости или совместимости, а не о том, что Bash "плохо портирован". Что сделать: Лучше переформулировать утверждение на более точное: «Bash-скрипт оказался не полностью переносимым, и некоторые Bash-специфичные функции не работали в другом окружении/шелле». Если нужен строгий фактологический чек, уточните, какие именно функции или какое окружение имеются в виду.

Фрагмент: >I've currently ported bash(1.08) and gcc(1.40), and things seem to work.
Обоснование:
Что найдено: The statement refers to a specific historical‑style port of `bash(1.08)` and `gcc(1.40)`, which roughly corresponds to early 1990s releases of Bash and GCC. However, modern practice and coding standards assume much later versions (e.g., Bash ≥3.x/4.x and GCC ≥4.x/5.x+); the cited numbers (1.08, 1.40) are not standard version strings in current documentation and may reflect custom or symbolic tags in a project rather than widely recognized releases. The claim itself is plausible as a project‑specific statement about porting legacy-compatible tools, but it lacks verifiable, up‑to‑date corroboration and is not objectively false.
Что сделать: Treat this claim as plausible in context but recommend clarifying which specific versions or forks of bash and gcc are meant (e.g., by referencing upstream release histories or changelogs) and flagging that these are early‑era versions not aligned with current mainstream distributions.

Фрагмент: Ориентируйся на стандарт POSIX.1-2017
Обоснование:
Что найдено: Последним официально опубликованным стандартом POSIX на 2026 г. является POSIX.1-2017, однако некоторые реализации и документация всё ещё ссылаются на более старые версии; фраза частично верна, но требует уточнения актуальности целевого профиля (например, system interfaces или shell utilities).
Что сделать: Уточнить, какой именно раздел POSIX.1-2017 (например, Shell and Utilities, Base Definitions) является базовым для проекта, чтобы студенты не опирались на устаревшие элементы стандартов.

Фрагмент: It is NOT portable (uses 386 task switching etc), and it probably never will support anything other than AT-harddisks, as that's all I have :-(.
Обоснование:
Что найдено: The cited fragment reflects the author’s (Linus Torvalds) opinion at the time of writing (early days of Linux) that the kernel was not portable because it relied on 386 task switching and AT hard disks and that it probably would never support other hardware. Subsequent history shows that Linux adopted strong portability and runs on many architectures (ARM, PowerPC, MIPS, etc.) and various storage subsystems, contradicting the ‘never will’ prediction. However, the quoted statement itself is historically accurate as an expression of the author’s view at that time.
Что сделать: Keep the quotation as‑is in the educational context since it authentically represents a historical viewpoint, but add a brief note explaining that Linux later became highly portable across many architectures and hardware types, so learners do not interpret the statement as currently true.

Фрагмент: >I'm doing a (free) operating system (just a hobby, won't be big and professional like gnu) for 386(486) AT clones.

Что найдено: The quoted statement is an excerpt from Linus Torvalds’s original announcement of what later became Linux, in which he compares his hobby project to MINIX and explicitly states that his operating system ‘resembles’ MINIX and shares the same physical layout of the file system. MINIX is correctly characterized as a Unix‑like, microkernel‑based OS created by Andrew Tanenbaum for educational use, but the claim focuses on the autobiographical/historical wording used by Torvalds rather than on a technical feature of MINIX itself. The phrasing is accurate as a quotation, but without context it may be misinterpreted as a general description of MINIX rather than a specific 1991 message from Torvalds.
Что сделать: Clarify that this text is a direct historical quotation from Linus Torvalds’s first Linux announcement (1991) and not a general description of MINIX, to avoid misleading readers about MINIX’s scope or purpose.
